channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Iranians are back online after a monthslong shutdown but face heavy restrictions
item.description: Iranians began to regain internet access after authorities ended a monthslong shutdown. Users said service was slow and spotty in some areas, with apps like YouTube and Instagram heavily restricted.
item.content:encoded:
Iranians began to regain internet access after authorities ended a monthslong shutdown. Users said service was slow and spotty in some areas, with apps like YouTube and Instagram heavily restricted.

(Image credit: Vahid Salemi)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Greetings from Cape Verde, where the sounds of samba, jazz and morna fill the air
item.description: Music is interwoven with the sounds of daily life in this West African island nation, which hosted two international music festivals in April and has been named the African Capital of Culture for 2028.
item.content:encoded: Music is interwoven with the sounds of daily life in this West African island nation, which hosted two international music festivals in April and has been named the African Capital of Culture for 2028.
item.dc:creator: Ricci Shryock

item.title: Palestinians use recycling as Israel's restrictions trigger a trash crisis
item.description: Palestinians in the West Bank live amid garbage following Israeli restrictions. Two Palestinian entrepreneurs are trying to make a change.
item.content:encoded:
Palestinians in the West Bank live amid garbage following Israeli restrictions. Two Palestinian entrepreneurs are trying to make a change.

(Image credit: Eleanor Beardsley)
item.dc:creator: Eleanor Beardsley

item.title: U.S. conducts another strike against Iran after Trump says Iran is 'negotiating on fumes'
item.description: U.S. forces have carried out new defensive strikes on an Iran military facility after downing Iranian attack drones. That's according to U.S. officials who were not authorized to comment publicly and spoke on the condition of anonymity.
item.content:encoded:
U.S. forces have carried out new defensive strikes on an Iran military facility after downing Iranian attack drones. That's according to U.S. officials who were not authorized to comment publicly and spoke on the condition of anonymity.

(Image credit: Jacquelyn Martin)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Why the French Open is named after Roland Garros, who didn't play tennis
item.description: Roland Garros, a WWI-era pilot, is credited with revolutionizing aerial combat. The major Paris tennis tournament was named after him in 1928, a decade after his death in combat.
item.content:encoded:
Roland Garros, a WWI-era pilot, is credited with revolutionizing aerial combat. The major Paris tennis tournament was named after him in 1928, a decade after his death in combat.

(Image credit: Branger/Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: An exceptionally early heat wave shatters records and brings deaths in Europe
item.description: Temperature records are breaking and triggering government warnings, with London hitting 95 degrees the past two days. Experts say unpredictable and extreme weather is becoming more frequent.
item.content:encoded:
Temperature records are breaking and triggering government warnings, with London hitting 95 degrees the past two days. Experts say unpredictable and extreme weather is becoming more frequent.

(Image credit: Kin Cheung)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Prospects fade for imminent end to Iran war as attacks restart
item.description: Israel says it will intensify attacks against Hezbollah in Lebanon and U.S. military struck Iranian boats and missile launch sites as envoys continued negotiations for a deal that would end the three-month war.
item.content:encoded:
Israel says it will intensify attacks against Hezbollah in Lebanon and U.S. military struck Iranian boats and missile launch sites as envoys continued negotiations for a deal that would end the three-month war.

(Image credit: AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Jane Arraf

item.title: U.S. strikes Iran. And, immigration courts use new tactic to speed up deportations
item.description: The U.S. military has launched new attacks on Iran while talks to end the conflict are ongoing. And, the Department of Justice is using a new tactic in immigration courts to accelerate deportations.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. military has launched new attacks on Iran while talks to end the conflict are ongoing. And, the Department of Justice is using a new tactic in immigration courts to accelerate deportations.

(Image credit: Michael M. Santiago)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Ecuadorian survivors of suspected drone strike seek answers and restitution
item.description: Survivors of a suspected drone strike while fishing in the Eastern Pacific seek restitution after claiming they were forced onto a boat and handed over to Salvadoran officials, with no charges filed.
item.content:encoded: Survivors of a suspected drone strike while fishing in the Eastern Pacific seek restitution after claiming they were forced onto a boat and handed over to Salvadoran officials, with no charges filed.
item.dc:creator: Carrie Kahn

item.title: Russia threatens more strikes against Kyiv after drone and missile attack
item.description: As Kyiv recovers from a Russian attack that hit every district in the Ukrainian capital, Ukraine continues long-range strikes on Russia's war machine even as the Kremlin threatens more strikes.
item.content:encoded: As Kyiv recovers from a Russian attack that hit every district in the Ukrainian capital, Ukraine continues long-range strikes on Russia's war machine even as the Kremlin threatens more strikes.
item.dc:creator: Joanna Kakissis
